source_pr.title: session: refactor non-transactional DML to make it ready for more DML types
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: ekexium <eke@fastmail.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #33485

Problem Summary:

To support `update` and `insert into select` later, first refactor the existing code specific to `delete`.

### What is changed and how it works?

1. Refactor
2. A proof-of-concept implementation of non-transactional update to show the refactor works.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: Non-transactional DML refactor + new tests for batch update/delete dry-run query output; no concrete flaky failure signature is captured in this case.
failure_signature: No explicit flaky failure signature in patch (refactor + tests).
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: The patch is a refactor/feature extension for non-transactional DML (to support more DML types) and adds/updates unit tests, including assertions on generated dry-run SQL (ORDER BY sharding key). It does not provide a concrete intermittent flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization fix, so keep this case as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: Refactor non-transactional DML and add/update unit tests (including dry-run SQL generation).

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("batch on a limit 3 dry run query update t set b = b + 42").Check(testkit.Rows("SELECT `a` FROM `test`.`t` WHERE TRUE ORDER BY IF(ISNULL(`a`),0,1),`a`")) (session/nontransactional_test.go)

files: DEPS.bzl, parser/ast/dml.go, parser/parser.go, parser/parser.y, parser/parser_test.go, planner/core/plan_cache_utils.go, planner/core/planbuilder.go, server/driver_tidb.go, session/nontransactional.go, session/nontransactional_test.go, testkit/testkit.go
